items.title: Iran deal presents political nightmare for Netanyahu
items.summary: Donald Trump's ceasefire agreement with Iran leaves the Israeli PM trapped in a new political and security dilemma.

items.title: Russian artist and Putin critic shot dead in Poland
items.summary: Robert Kuzovkov, who used the pseudonym Semyon Skrepetsky, has been known for his caricatures of politicians including Vladimir Putin.

items.title: Eight dead after US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes in California
items.summary: The incident occurred on Monday morning while the aircraft had been on a routine test mission.

items.title: Cuba tourism collapses as US pressure campaign bites
items.summary: The number of foreign visitors is down by 58% compared to last year, Cuban officials say, amid sanctions and an effective oil blockade.

items.title: Hungary's MPs block return of Orbán, limiting rule of PM to eight years
items.summary: The vote fulfils a promise by new PM Péter Magyar to stop his predecessor from becoming prime minister again.

items.title: 'Dancing girl's' bare torso restored in Indian textbook after backlash
items.summary: A picture in a new school textbook had covered up the naked torso of the famous figurine with dark shading.

items.title: Japan raises interest rate to highest for 31 years
items.summary: The Bank of Japan has been raising rates from near-zero since 2024.

items.title: India temporarily bans Telegram over exam paper leak concerns
items.summary: The NEET exam had earlier been scrapped over allegations of a paper leak, sparking massive protests.

items.title: California's Gavin Newsom alleges justice department is investigating his wife and ex-staff
items.summary: A source familiar with the investigations says they had been ongoing for "roughly a year".

items.title: Tunisia sack head coach just one game into World Cup
items.summary: Tunisia sack Sabri Lamouchi one game into their World Cup, with former Morocco and Saudi Arabia manager Herve Renard replacing him.

items.title: Nigerian army frees widow of ex-general who died in captivity
items.summary: Maj Gen Rabe Abubakar and his wife Amina were abducted in north-west Nigeria at the end of last month.

items.title: Married at First Sight Australia allegations 'disturbing', says country's watchdog
items.summary: The claims also prompted a response from UK media regulator Ofcom, who called them "deeply concerning".

items.title: Australia to probe assault claims by Gaza flotilla activists against Israeli forces
items.summary: Australian activists claim they were kidnapped, raped and tortured after being detained in May.

items.title: Brazil woman dies after rope-jumping instructors fail to attach cord
items.summary: Three men have been arrested after instructors failed to attach a rope to her before helping her jump from a bridge.

items.title: Trust in news hits a new low, research suggests
items.summary: The Reuters Institute records its lowest trust figures since the annual research began in 2015.

items.title: Norwegian crown princess's son found guilty of two counts of rape
items.summary: Marius Borg Høiby, the 29-year-old son of Norway's Crown Princess Mette Marit, has been sentenced to four years in jail.

items.title: South African jazz legend Abdullah Ibrahim dies at 91
items.summary: Ibrahim helped define a genre of South African jazz music in a career that spanned eight decades.

items.title: MSF staff abused Sudanese refugees in sex-for-food scandal
items.summary: Some victims said they chose not to speak out because they feared staff would cut off access to aid.

items.title: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates
items.summary: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?

items.title: 'Greatest feeling ever': Cape Verdeans tell BBC of joy at holding Spain to draw
items.summary: The streets of the capital, Praia, shook with wild celebrations as the small island nation held Spain to a shock 0-0 draw.

items.title: Recovery of Ebola patients offers rare moments of joy at epicentre of outbreak
items.summary: There are glimpses of happiness in the Democratic Republic of Congo's fight against the virus that has killed more than 170.

items.title: A year on, six questions still haunt the Air India crash investigation
items.summary: From fuel switches to engine failures, here are the biggest mysteries around the Air India crash inquiry.

items.title: Bowen: Iran deal ends Trump's war that revealed limit of US dominance
items.summary: The deal to end fighting and re-open the Strait of Hormuz leaves the sides where they were 24 hours before the war - only with thousands now dead.

items.title: Watch: California wildfires rage near passing vehicles
items.summary: The fires in Riverside County cover over 2,000 acres, say authorities.

items.title: Iranian-Americans protest against Iran team at World Cup
items.summary: The BBC’s Shaimaa Khalil was outside the Iran v New Zealand opening round match as protesters called for an end to Tehran’s clerical regime.

items.title: What did Trump do differently to Obama on Iran?
items.summary: The BBC's Gary O'Donoghue looks at the contrasting approaches of the two presidents as Trump touts a new peace deal.

items.title: Watch: What does the US-Iran deal to end war mean for Lebanon and Israel?
items.summary: It's not clear if the deal means the Israelis will pause their offensive in Lebanon, BBC international editor Jeremy Bowen reports.
